title: My Funded Futures Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026)

excerpt: Complete guide to setting up and using My Funded Futures — from account creation to pro-level tips.
content:
What is My Funded Futures?

My Funded Futures is a prop firm specializing exclusively in futures trading, founded in 2023 and headquartered in Dover, Delaware. The platform offers traders a simplified path to capital: complete a one-phase evaluation on paper money, and if you pass, receive up to 90% profit splits on a funded account. With a 4.4/5 rating and starting prices from $77/month, it's built specifically for futures day traders and algo traders seeking fast payouts—some funded accounts receive their first payout in just 15–20 minutes. Unlike legacy competitors that charge $149–$300+ in activation fees, My Funded Futures eliminates those upfront costs entirely, making it an accessible entry point for traders ready to scale their edge.

How to Create Your My Funded Futures Account

Account creation takes approximately 10–15 minutes and requires basic personal information and trading verification. Here's the process:

  • Visit the signup page – Go to My Funded Futures' website and click the registration button. You'll enter your email, create a password, and provide your full name.
  • Identity verification – Upload a government-issued ID (passport, driver's license, or national ID) and proof of address (utility bill, bank statement, or lease dated within the last 90 days). This typically takes 24 hours to verify.
  • Trading questionnaire – Answer basic questions about your trading experience, strategy focus (day trading, swing trading, algo), and account size preference. This determines which evaluation plan matches your needs.
  • Select your evaluation tier – Choose from Core, Rapid, or Pro plans with $50K, $100K, or $150K starting capital. Your choice here depends on your trading style and risk tolerance (more on this below).
  • Payment method – Add a credit card, debit card, or bank transfer. My Funded Futures processes monthly subscription fees in advance; if you pass the evaluation, your next month's fee is waived or applied toward your funded account setup.
  • Accept terms and begin evaluation – Review and sign the trading agreement. You'll then receive login credentials and immediate access to the paper trading dashboard within minutes.

Verification timeline: Most traders are fully approved within 24–48 hours. You can start paper trading immediately while your documents are being verified. No activation fees means no surprise charges—only the monthly subscription cost applies.

Setting Up My Funded Futures for the First Time

Once your account is approved, you'll land on the main dashboard. Here's what to configure:

  • Dashboard overview – The homepage displays your account balance, equity, P&L, win rate, and current drawdown in real time. Unlike competitors focused on stocks or forex, My Funded Futures shows futures-specific metrics (contract exposure, rollover status, daily P&L).
  • Broker integration – My Funded Futures partners with Tradovate for paper trading execution. Link your Tradovate account (or create a new one—it's free) to sync orders from the platform. This integration is automatic; you authorize it once and orders execute in both your paper and live accounts.
  • Risk settings – Set your daily loss limit and maximum drawdown threshold. For example, Core accounts typically allow a 5% daily drawdown; Pro accounts with "no consistency rules" allow much larger swings. Configure these in the Settings menu under Risk Management.
  • Performance analytics dashboard – Explore the "Analytics" tab to view trade history, win/loss ratios, win rate percentage, and average profit per trade. This data tracks your performance against the evaluation rules in real time, so you know exactly where you stand.
  • Notifications – Enable email or SMS alerts for trade execution, daily P&L reports, and drawdown warnings. This keeps you informed if you're approaching your loss limits.
  • Algo/automation settings – If you trade with a bot or EA (expert advisor), My Funded Futures supports automated trading. Configure API access in the "Integrations" section and link your algo directly to your evaluation account.

The setup is intentionally streamlined compared to platforms like TopStep (which requires additional compliance modules) or Funded Trader Plus (which has more elaborate onboarding). My Funded Futures gets you trading within 30 minutes of login.

Essential Features You Should Know

1. Paper Trading with Zero Cost – Your entire evaluation happens on paper money, not real capital. You trade using the same Tradovate interface and live order execution as funded traders, so there's no learning curve between evaluation and live trading. Mistakes during your 30-day evaluation cost you nothing; you simply reset and try again if you exceed your drawdown limit.

2. Broker Integration (Tradovate) – My Funded Futures' partnership with Tradovate means your charts, order routing, and fill speeds are identical to live futures trading. You're not learning on a simulator; you're learning on the real platform. This eliminates surprises when you graduate to a funded account.

3. Automated Trading Support – Unlike stock-focused prop firms that restrict algos, My Funded Futures actively supports automated trading. You can connect NinjaTrader, ThinkorSwim, or custom Python scripts via API. Your trades execute in real time against live futures prices, giving your bot a genuine test environment.

4. Performance Analytics – Every trade is logged with entry price, exit price, time held, profit/loss, and market conditions. The analytics dashboard aggregates this into win rate, profit factor, Sharpe ratio, and max consecutive winners/losers. You can filter by contract (ES, MES, YM, etc.) to pinpoint which markets your edge works best in.

5. Risk Management Rules – Your evaluation tier determines your risk boundaries. Core accounts allow a 5% daily loss limit; Pro accounts remove consistency rules entirely, letting you risk larger amounts on fewer, higher-conviction trades. The platform enforces these limits automatically—if you hit your daily max, trading stops until the next day.

6. Fast Payout System – Once you pass, funded traders report receiving their first payout in 15–20 minutes. This is significantly faster than competitors like Phase Evaluation (3–5 business days) or legacy firms (5–10 days). Profits are transferred via bank transfer or check, available immediately.

7. Clear Path to Live Trading – After 5 consecutive monthly payouts on your funded account, you're eligible for a live trading account with real capital. This progression gives underperforming traders a concrete milestone rather than leaving them in evaluation limbo indefinitely.

My Funded Futures Pricing: Which Plan Should You Choose?

My Funded Futures offers three evaluation tiers across three account sizes. Here's the breakdown:

  • Core Plans – $50K account for $77/month. Best for beginners testing their strategy and risk tolerance. Lower capital means smaller position sizes and lower stress, but also lower max profit potential. Core accounts maintain consistency rules (daily loss limits, max drawdown percentages).
  • Rapid Plans – $50K ($129/mo), $100K ($229/mo), or $150K ($377/mo). "Rapid" signifies a slightly faster evaluation process and higher profit potential. Rapid accounts are ideal for intermediate traders with a proven strategy and solid win rate. You'll pay more per month but access larger position sizes.
  • Pro Plans – $50K ($229/mo), $100K ($329/mo), or $150K ($377/mo). Pro is the flagship tier, featuring no consistency rules on funded accounts. This means no daily loss limits once you're funded—you can take one large trade if your edge justifies it. Pro is built for experienced traders running algos, running large accounts, or trading high-conviction setups.

Price comparison: A $100K Rapid account costs $229/month versus TopStep's $299/month for equivalent capital. A $100K Pro account (no consistency rules) typically costs more at legacy firms ($349–$399/mo), making My Funded Futures competitive on pricing and feature set.

Which to choose? Start with Core ($77/mo) if you're new to funded trading or testing a new strategy. Step up to Rapid ($129–$377/mo) if you're profitable on your own dime and ready to scale. Choose Pro ($229–$377/mo) only if consistency rules actively constrain your edge—many profitable traders succeed under Core and Rapid rules and prefer the lower monthly cost.

No tier offers a free trial; you must commit to the monthly subscription. However, the $77 Core entry point is the lowest among major competitors, and there are no hidden activation or setup fees (a savings of $150–$300+ versus TopStep, 5ive, or Funded Trader Plus).

Pro Tips for Getting the Most Out of My Funded Futures

1. Leverage Tradovate's built-in features during evaluation. My Funded Futures integrates Tradovate's advanced charting, volume profile, and market replay tools directly. Before evaluation, spend 2–3 weeks using Tradovate's free tier to familiarize yourself with order types, hotkeys, and the DOM (depth of market). Your evaluation will feel natural, not rushed.

2. Test your exact algorithm before starting. If you trade a bot, run it live (on paper Tradovate) for at least 10 days before paying for an evaluation. My Funded Futures' evaluation only lasts 30 days; a bug in your algo discovered halfway through wastes your $77–$377 monthly fee. A 10-day pre-test reveals issues upfront.

3. Use the no-consistency-rules on Pro as a strategic advantage. Pro accounts allow you to hold overnight positions without daily P&L restrictions. If your edge is a swing or position trade (multi-day holds), Pro's flexibility is worth the extra $100–$148/month versus Rapid. You'll take fewer trades, lower stress, and potentially higher accuracy.

4. Document every evaluation attempt and metric. My Funded Futures' analytics dashboard records win rate, profit factor, and Sharpe ratio. If you fail an evaluation, export this data and analyze it offline (via spreadsheet or Python). Identifying whether you failed due to bad luck, insufficient sample size, or a flawed strategy ensures your second or third attempt succeeds. Most traders who fail once and adjust pass within 2–3 tries.

5. Join the futures contract that matches your account size. A $50K account trading ES (E-mini S&P 500, 1 contract = ~$3,000 notional) versus MES (Micro E-mini, 1 contract = ~$300 notional) will feel very different. Use MES early to manage risk; graduate to ES only when you're profitable. My Funded Futures' analytics let you backtest position sizing before live evaluation.

6. Exploit the 15–20 minute payout window once funded. Other firms take days to pay; My Funded Futures transfers profits almost instantly. This means you can scale faster—prove consistent profitability in week 2, request your first payout by week 3, and use that capital to fund a second funded account in week 4. This reinvestment cycle compounds your funded capital quickly.

7. Clarify the 5-consecutive-payout requirement for live trading early. To graduate to a live account, you need 5 straight profitable months (no missed payouts). If you're inconsistent or break even a few times, the clock resets. Plan your evaluation and first few funded months accordingly; don't expect a live account until month 7–8 minimum.

Common My Funded Futures Issues and How to Fix Them

Issue 1: Evaluation account reset or revoked due to drawdown. You exceed your daily or max drawdown limit and your evaluation terminates. Fix: Review the analytics dashboard to identify your biggest losing trade. Was it a mistake, a system failure, or bad luck? If it was a mistake (wrong position size, missed stop loss), adjust your process. If it's a system failure (algo bug), fix and paper trade for 10 more days. If it's bad luck, try again—a 25% evaluation pass rate means 75% of traders fail at least once. Most pass on their second or third attempt with tighter risk management.

Issue 2: Slow payouts or missing profit transfer. You're funded and profitable, but your payout hasn't hit your bank account within the promised 15–20 minutes. Fix: Check the "Payouts" tab in your account dashboard first—it will show "Pending," "Processing," or "Completed." If it's stuck on "Processing" after 2 hours, contact support via email or chat. Delays are rare, but they happen during high-volume payout periods (month-end or during market volatility). Support typically resolves this within 4–8 hours.

Issue 3: Tradovate connectivity or order execution delays. Your orders are slow to fill, or the platform occasionally disconnects. Fix: Ensure your Tradovate account's API authentication is current (re-authorize in My Funded Futures' Integrations menu monthly). Check your internet connection—a wired Ethernet connection is more stable than WiFi for trading. Tradovate's servers occasionally lag during major market moves (FOMC announcements, large economic releases). If delays persist, switch to a hardwired connection and enable "direct routing" in Tradovate for faster execution.

Issue 4: Confusion about consistency rules on your specific tier. You fail an evaluation because you didn't realize Core and Rapid have daily/max drawdown rules, but you thought you didn't. Fix: Before starting, read the official rules document for your tier on the My Funded Futures website. Pro accounts remove consistency rules once funded; Core and Rapid maintain them throughout evaluation and funding. Screenshot these rules and post them next to your trading desk—no ambiguity.

Is My Funded Futures Worth It? Our Verdict

My Funded Futures is worth it for futures traders seeking zero activation fees, industry-leading payout speed, and a high evaluation pass rate (25%, nearly double the industry average). The $77–$229/month entry cost is competitive, and the simplified one-phase evaluation structure eliminates the exhausting multi-month qualification grind of legacy competitors. However, it's not suitable for stock traders, forex traders, or anyone needing a proprietary mobile app—its futures-only focus and reliance on Tradovate for execution are deliberate trade-offs. If you're a futures day trader or algo trader with a proven edge and capital goals between $50K and $150K, My Funded Futures is a strong choice. If you're learning or need flexibility across multiple asset classes, explore TopStep Trader (multi-asset) or 5ive Funded Trader (lower costs) instead.
